title: hieracium ramondii subsp. neochrysanthemum
intro: Hieracium ramondii subsp. neochrysanthemum, also known as Ramond's hawkweed, is a lesser-known, flowering plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Ramond's hawkweed include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, Ramond's hawkweed is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its widespread cultivation and usage are limited due to its rarity and potential toxicity.
